title: second-shift consumer-repo configuration (static context)
description: The single per-repo onboarding file (.claude/second-shift.config.json). Everything repo-specific that the generic plugins need: tracker, topology, base branches, command truth table, reviewer registry deltas, paths, gates. Domain knowledge does NOT belong here — it lives in repo-local skills and extension files (see docs/extension-points.md).

$schema.description: Optional editor-validation pointer; onboard writes the schema URL at the pinned marketplace ref.

configVersion.description: Schema version of this config file; bumped only on breaking schema changes.

writes.description: Whether the pipeline writes back to the tracker (stage comments, status/label transitions, claim). github default true (queue+claim+comment model); JIRA setups typically run read-only (fetch the ticket via MCP, never transition or comment — the state file + draft-PR metadata are the audit trail), so JIRA repos usually set false. A false value makes the tracker-write steps in stages 1/8/9 no-ops; ticket fetch stays active regardless.

branchPrefix.description: Branch-name namespace prepended to the ticket key. The work branch is "<branchPrefix><ticketKey>" for slice 1 and "<branchPrefix><ticketKey>-pr<N>" for stacked slice N. github example: "claude/acme-" (→ claude/acme-42). JIRA example: a per-user identifier plus a slash, e.g. "jdoe/" (→ jdoe/gh-540). Gives the previously-hardcoded prefix in max-pushed-slice.sh a config home.

bot.description: github only: dedicated bot identity for issue/PR writes (claim safety).

wrapperPath.description: Explicit path to the gh-as-bot wrapper script. When set, install-gh-bot.sh writes it here and claim-issue.sh's GH_BOT default resolves to it, instead of the $HOME/.config/<repo-basename>/gh-as-bot.sh derivation (which was derived wrong once — a canary catch). Supports $HOME/~ expansion.

app.description: GitHub App identity for the bot (previously hardcoded in install-gh-bot.sh with no config home). Onboarding a different repo's bot app sets these instead of editing script constants.

appName.description: GitHub App slug, e.g. "acme-dev-pipeline" (used in settings-page URLs and messages).

privateKeyFilename.description: Private-key filename installed under the bot config dir, e.g. "acme-dev-pipeline.private-key.pem".

installationId.description: Optional: pin the installation ID to skip API discovery.

keyPattern.description: Regex for ticket keys as used in branch names and state files (e.g. "[A-Z]+-[0-9]+" for JIRA, "[0-9]+" for GitHub issue numbers). statectl init validates the key against this anchored pattern (fails closed on a malformed key); absent = accept any non-empty key.

labels.description: github only: the queue/claim/blocker label vocabulary by ROLE (not a flat list). Every functional site reads these roles — the Stage-1 queue query, the claim label-swap, the do-not-pick-up guard, claim-issue.sh, and pipeline-doctor — and the pre-flight existence check verifies their union. Absent → the shipped defaults. Config home for the previously-hardcoded label literals (F75); a consumer with custom labels sets these once and every site follows.

queue.description: The label the pipeline picks work from and drops at claim. Default "ready-for-dev".

claimed.description: The label added atomically at claim (and removed at terminal stages). Default "in-progress".

blockers.description: Labels that block auto-pickup (an issue carrying any is skipped). Default ["epic","needs-intake-review","needs-spec-work","needs-plan-review"].

repos.description: Keyed by logical repo id ("be", "fe", or a single id for standalone/monorepo). The pipeline is always invoked from the repo this config lives in; sibling repos are reached via relative paths.

path.description: Relative to the config's repo root; "." for the host repo.

baseBranch.description: The integration mainline PRs target (repos in a pair may differ, e.g. BE alpha / FE main).

worktreesDir.description: Where pipeline worktrees are created for this repo (default ../<repo>-worktrees).

ticketTag.description: Ticket-title tag routing work to this repo (e.g. "[BE]").

commands.description: Command truth table per logical repo id (keys must exist in topology.repos). null = lane not available in this repo (verifyctl must not select it).

lintAutofixes.description: Whether the lint command mutates files (verifyctl LINT_AUTOFIX failure classification).

testFile.description: Template for running ONE spec file (the mutation gate's per-spec runner). {file} is replaced with the spec path; the command runs from the worktree root. e.g. "yarn --cwd apps/api test {file}". null = no per-spec runner (previously hardcoded to the acme apps/api form).

unitTestScope.description: Glob selecting the backend source in scope for the unit-test mutation gate (Stages 3-5 classify unitTestSurface against it). e.g. "apps/api/src/**". null = not scoped (the whole repo, or the gate is off).

lanes.description: Monorepo lane matrix: per-workspace command sets verifyctl expands (e.g. apps/*, packages/*).

extraLanes.description: EP-2: additive verify lanes. Run sequentially AFTER the built-in SUITE lanes, never interleaving or replacing them. Results land under namespaced ext:<name> keys in the verify summary (canonical keys unreachable). No advisory mode — a lane blocks Stage-6 completion or it does not exist.

name.description: kebab-case, unique; results recorded under ext:<name>.

when.description: Changed-file globs gating execution; empty/absent = always run. An inert diff skips extra lanes too.

failureClass.description: MUST be an existing closed failure-taxonomy value — extensions borrow the taxonomy, never extend it; gets the standard 2-attempt budget.

reviewers.description: Deltas against the plugin-shipped review-lead registry.

add.description: Repo-local reviewer agents (in this repo's .claude/agents/) to register with review-lead.

dimensions.description: Review dimensions this agent covers (dedup/routing hint for review-lead).

remove.description: Plugin-shipped reviewers to disable in this repo (e.g. db-reviewer in an FE repo).

modelOverrides.description: Per-agent model-tier override, keyed by any agent the .mjs dispatch tables model — panel reviewers AND named workflow agents like mutation-executor (EP-4). Observed need: security-reviewer opus in one repo, sonnet in another. check-model-tiers validates .mjs tables against frontmatter + these overrides.

plansDir.description: Directory (worktree-relative) where Stage 3 writes plan files. Default = the plugin's current literal; a consumer that sets it has it honored (Stage 3 reads the key — fixed the prior drift where it was ignored).

gates.description: Optional pipeline gates/capabilities. Everything defaults to off; a gate that is on but missing its prerequisites must fail closed at run time.

costTracking.description: Local OTel cost estimation (requires operator-side collector setup).

design.description: Optional design-fidelity axis. Selects the design-toolkit adapter family. Key absent = design fidelity off (a run behaves exactly as a non-design run). Prerequisites missing at run time (Figma MCP for figma; DesignSync for claude-design) fail closed, same posture as every gate.

provider.description: figma → figma-faithful skills + Figma-MCP prerequisite; claude-design → design-faithful/design-sync path + DesignSync prerequisite.

stageParams.description: Optional stage-prose constants promoted to config. Every key is optional and defaults to the plugin's current shipped literal, so an empty config reproduces today's behavior byte-for-byte. Pure parameterization — no ordering, no logic.

planFilePattern.description: Plan file path pattern. Tokens: {plansDir} (from paths.plansDir), {issueKey}, {slice} (e.g. -pr2, empty for single-PR).

requiredLabels.description: Tracker labels the queue-and-claim model requires. Default = the plugin's shipped set (ready-for-dev, needs-spec-work, needs-plan-review, needs-intake-review, in-progress, epic).

visualCapture.description: Stage-6 render-surface visual capture. Defaults reproduce the shipped literals.

viewports.description: Closed enum of named viewports; the stage maps each name to its pixel dimensions.

triggerGlobs.description: Render-surface globs that gate visual capture. Default = the shipped apps/web component/CSS/tailwind globs.

stageWorkflows.description: EP-6: gate-owned workflow dispatch. Registers a Workflow script (a companion plugin's, or repo-local) as a BLOCKING stage sub-step — dispatched AFTER the stage's built-in sub-steps and BEFORE the stage-completion write. Registration lives in consumer config (auditable, per-repo), never in the plugin manifest. Always blocking (no advisory field — advisory lanes don't exist). Result recorded under stageCheckpoint[N].extWorkflows[<name>]; a failure produces the stage's standard fail-fast write with reason ext-workflow-failed (the name in the detail field). The workflow may write state ONLY via statectl checkpoint payloads namespaced ext: (adds evidence, never reinterprets it). Unresolvable reference = config-lint failure, fail closed.

name.description: kebab-case, unique across stageWorkflows; results recorded under this name.

workflow.description: "<plugin>:<relpath>" (a companion plugin's workflow script) or a repo-relative path.

implementDelegates.description: EP-7: implementer delegation (Stage 5). Routes matching work items to a delegate agent INSTEAD of inline implementation; the delegate's output passes through the unchanged Stage-5 scope-enforcement gate and all downstream gates (adds work, waives nothing). Unresolvable agent = fail closed at pre-flight.

surface.description: A path glob, or the reserved surface key 'unit'. Work items matching it route to the delegate instead of inline implementation.

agent.description: "<plugin>:<agent>" (a companion pack agent) or a repo-local bare agent name (in .claude/agents/).

planGates.description: EP-8: additive Stage-4 plan-review gates. Registers a consumer/companion plan-reviewer agent that runs AFTER the built-in plan gates (plan-reviewer, design FE-spec, unit-test-plan) and returns a trinary verdict — additive-only: it can only make a passing plan-review block, never waive a built-in gate. A block maps to the existing plan-reviewer-block reason (no per-extension reason). Symmetric with commands.<repo>.extraLanes (Stage-6 verify) and reviewers.add (Stage-8 code review).

name.description: kebab-case, unique; the gate ledger key is plan-gate:<name>.

surface.description: Optional path glob — Stage 4 runs the gate only when the plan touches this surface (absent = always).

agent.description: "<plugin>:<agent>" (companion pack) or a repo-local bare agent name (in .claude/agents/). Unresolvable = fail closed at pre-flight.
